findings.description: Incremental sliding-window sum accumulates floating-point rounding error over long inputs, unlike a full recompute per window.
insort into a list is O(n) per add, so the class named StreamingMedian is O(n^2) overall despite implying efficient streaming.
safe_mean does no NaN/inf guarding, so a single NaN value silently poisons the mean (likely acceptable for the domain).
clamp passes NaN through unchanged via min/max rather than rejecting it, which may surprise callers.
typing.Optional is used while PEP 585 list[float] generics are used elsewhere; X | None would be more consistent.
